title: Media Types, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity
description: The parser consumes a partial compressed response, guesses platform encoding, ignores conflicting declarations, and hashes normalized text as source bytes. Learners cumulatively produce byte-to-Unicode admission ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Validate declared content codings and distinguish unsupported, corrupt, truncated, and over-budget representations.
Use a documented media-type and conservative sniff policy while rejecting binary, multipart, and ambiguous content.
Reconcile transport, BOM, HTML declarations, decoder support, replacement characters, and confidence with source offsets.
